name: Textbook max-flow (unit Day 2)
description: The six-node worked example from the flow-networks unit. Edmonds-Karp finds maximum flow 14 (s->A->C->t pushes 4, s->B->D->t pushes 9, then s->A->D->t rebalances 1 across the residual A->D edge). The minimum cut also has capacity 14, certifying optimality. Modeled here with s as the sole supply and t as the sole demand; supply/demand quantities are set high so the lane capacities are what bind.
